dependencies: Single or limited number of suppliers for certain businesses
Server processor technology for certain semiconductors
Reliance on suppliers for components, supplies, services and raw materials
risks: Changes in business condition of suppliers could subject company to losses
Failure of suppliers to deliver components in sufficient quantities or timely manner
Defective components or materials from suppliers could reduce product reliability

Supply chain disruptions from severe weather events, prolonged temperature changes, natural disasters, climate change impacts, uncertain political climates, international hostilities, terrorist activities, pandemics

regulatory_risks: Development and enforcement of laws and regulations specifically targeted at technology industry
U.S. and foreign data privacy requirements
Cybersecurity, data protection and privacy laws and regulations
International data transfer mechanisms and data location requirements
Increased cybersecurity requirements and reporting obligations
Carbon taxes and new regulations affecting hardware products and data centers
Environmental laws and regulations including U.S. Superfund law
Premiums for mandatory pension insolvency insurance coverage outside the United States
Regulations on security of critical infrastructure and IoT devices
Customer industry regulations (e.g., financial services, healthcare)

sanction_risks: International trade disputes could create uncertainty
Tariffs and international trade sanctions could affect ability to move goods and services across borders
Measures to mitigate trade sanctions or tariffs could be made less effective if sanctions or tariffs change
